metadata.source: Per-region dossiers (accessibility.md / regen.md / stability.md / climate.md); each entry carries its primary citation where available in its `source` property
metadata.license: Research dossiers internal; underlying primary sources carry their own licenses
metadata.note: Delivers the Spec Step 1 explicit gap-candidate (Overview Open Q3 / Spec Step 1 top-down review). Population/demographic dimensions added under metric #1 as r4 proposed.

properties.population_trend_notes: Alentejo region population trajectory 2011-2021 was -6.9% per INE Censos 2021, with persistent rural depopulation partially offset by inbound EU retirees and lifestyle migrants since 2018.
properties.median_age_notes: Median age ~50 years in 2023, oldest of Portugal's NUTS-II regions per Pordata.
properties.migration_notes: Net rural decline offset by inbound migration of EU retirees, French/German/Dutch/Belgian lifestyle migrants and digital nomads, especially in Alto and Baixo Alentejo coastal strip.
properties.rural_density_notes: Alentejo population density ~22 inhabitants/km2 in 2023, roughly one-eighth Portugal's national average of ~111/km2.
properties.source: accessibility.md, INE Portugal Censos 2021 and Pordata 2023

properties.population_trend_notes: Interior Ourense province has lost ~15% of population since 2000 per IGE; net negative for interior with coastal areas stable.
properties.median_age_notes: Median age ~49.5 years in 2023, Spain's oldest NUTS-II region by median age per INE 2023.
properties.migration_notes: Net negative for interior; positive net migration into coastal towns; neorrural return-to-village movement statistically marginal but locally visible.
properties.rural_density_notes: Galicia overall density ~91 inhabitants/km2 in 2023, close to Spain national 95/km2, but bimodal with coastal arc dense and interior Ourense/Lugo at depopulation-frontier densities.

properties.population_trend_notes: Romania national rural population trajectory 2011-2021 was -9.6% per INS Census 2021; many rural villages have lost 30-50% of population since 1990 while Cluj-Napoca grows.
properties.median_age_notes: Median age in rural Transylvania 2022: ~46 years; in Cluj-Napoca 2022: ~38 years.
properties.migration_notes: Dominant emigration outflow (~3-4 million Romanians abroad); modest return migration post-2020; Saxon-village foreign-resident pockets in Sibiu and Brasov counties grew through 2023-2024.
properties.rural_density_notes: Centru and Nord-Vest NUTS-II regions density ~70 inhabitants/km2 per 2022 Census, with Cluj concentration and thin rural backcountry.

properties.population_trend_notes: West Cork experienced significant in-migration through 2020-2024; Connemara Gaeltacht core declining slowly but supported by Udaras na Gaeltachta investment.
properties.median_age_notes: Median age in rural West Cork 2022: ~42 years; Connemara Gaeltacht core: ~43 years per CSO Census 2022.
properties.migration_notes: West Cork net positive driven by remote-work and lifestyle migration; Connemara Gaeltacht core net negative slowly.
properties.rural_density_notes: West Cork ~29 inhabitants/km2 and Connemara/Galway West ~22 inhabitants/km2 per CSO Census 2022, vs Ireland national ~73/km2.

properties.population_trend_notes: Pembrokeshire population stable-to-slightly-declining since 2011; coastal areas see seasonal and retirement in-migration; rural inland wards lose young adults.
properties.median_age_notes: Median age in Pembrokeshire 2021 Census: ~46 years vs Wales 42, UK 40.
properties.migration_notes: Coastal towns receive in-migration from across the UK and (post-Brexit) reduced inflow from continental Europe; rural inland wards lose young adults.
properties.rural_density_notes: Pembrokeshire ~78 inhabitants/km2 per 2021 Census; rural ward density materially lower at 10-30/km2.

properties.population_trend_notes: Net positive migration into Lozere and rural Gard 2015-2024 driven by remote workers, retirees, and neo-ruraux; population growth in Lozere reversed long-term decline modestly.
properties.median_age_notes: Median age in Lozere 2024: ~46 years per INSEE 2024.
properties.migration_notes: Cevennes-area communes show positive net migration since 2015 per INSEE migration data 2020-2024; neo-rural wave continues.
properties.rural_density_notes: Lozere ~15 inhabitants/km2 in 2024, France's lowest departement density; National Park des Cevennes ~24/km2 across 2,930 km2.

properties.population_trend_notes: Population growth 2011 to 2023: +5.3% per ASTAT, positive trajectory unusual for European mountain regions.
properties.median_age_notes: Median age 2024: ~43 years, younger than Italy national ~47 and rural-Italy norm per ASTAT 2024.
properties.migration_notes: Positive net migration including from Italy, Germany, Austria, and increasingly from Eastern Europe.
properties.rural_density_notes: South Tirol ~72 inhabitants/km2 in 2024 (~535,000 across ~7,400 km2) per ASTAT.

properties.population_trend_notes: Population 2011 to 2023: -6.4% per INE/SADEI; steep depopulation in interior valleys and Cantabrian mountains; birth rate among Europe's lowest.
properties.median_age_notes: Median age Asturias 2024: ~50.4 years, the oldest of Spain's 17 autonomous communities per INE 2024.
properties.migration_notes: Net negative overall; modest inflow of urban-fleeing repobladores, retirees, and Latin American (especially Venezuelan and Colombian) migrants since 2018, not enough to reverse decline.
properties.rural_density_notes: Asturias regional density ~95 inhabitants/km2 in 2023 but heavily concentrated in Oviedo-Gijon-Aviles belt; rural-interior comarca densities 10-30/km2.

properties.population_trend_notes: Lost population continuously since 1990; population 2011 to 2023: -4.5% per LSA 2024, with continued decline projected.
properties.median_age_notes: Median age 2024: ~48 years, among Germany's oldest per Destatis 2024.
properties.migration_notes: Continuous net out-migration since 1990; modest stabilisation post-2015 in metropolitan Halle/Magdeburg; rural depopulation continues; foreign-resident share remains low (~7%) but growing.
properties.rural_density_notes: State ~106 inhabitants/km2 in 2024; rural Landkreis density e.g. Altmarkkreis Salzwedel: ~32/km2 per LSA 2024.

properties.population_trend_notes: Estonia's overall population stabilised post-2017 after long decline (return migration of diaspora plus Ukrainian refugees post-2022); rural Setomaa continues to decline; Saaremaa stable or modestly positive.
properties.median_age_notes: Median age Estonia 2024: ~42 years per Statistikaamet 2024.
properties.migration_notes: Return migration of diaspora plus Ukrainian refugees post-2022 stabilised national population; Saaremaa sees modest in-migration of remote workers; Setomaa declines.
properties.rural_density_notes: Estonia national density ~30 inhabitants/km2 in 2024; Saaremaa ~12/km2 (~30,000 people on ~2,673 km2); Setomaa parish density ~5-7/km2.

properties.population_trend_notes: Oregon's population grew 10.6% 2010-2020 per US Census 2020, driven primarily by net in-migration from California; Lane, Benton, and Linn counties all recorded positive net migration 2015-2022.
properties.median_age_notes: Median age in Oregon 2022: 39.7 years per US Census ACS 2022, younger than EU peer regions with a significantly younger in-migration stream.
properties.migration_notes: Net-attracting younger, educated migrants from California and across the US; climate-haven migration noted; not building into an ageing, shrinking community but an arriving one.
properties.rural_density_notes: Willamette Valley regional average ~55 persons/km2 per JRC GHSL R2023; rural hinterland 5-30/km2 with urban cores (Salem ~2,000/km2, Eugene ~1,800/km2).
properties.source: accessibility.md, JRC GHSL R2023 and US Census 2020

properties.population_trend_notes: Vermont is the second least-populated state in the US after Wyoming; population is ageing and the rural workforce is thin; back-to-the-land migration history (35,000+ new rural settlers 1968-1975) seeded persistent regen culture.
properties.migration_notes: Climate-haven and back-to-the-land migration; the 1970s counterculture wave seeded durable infrastructure; new wave of climate-aware migrants continuing.
properties.rural_density_notes: Vermont statewide population density 27 persons/km2 per US Census 2020 (49th lowest state); highly rural counties Essex 9/km2 and Orleans 15/km2; agricultural/forested interior of central Vermont ~5-15 persons/km2.
properties.source: accessibility.md, US Census Bureau 2020 Decennial Census
properties.gaps: Dossier does not give an explicit Vermont median age number, only references ageing population qualitatively.

properties.population_trend_notes: Climate-haven and back-to-the-land migration into Western North Carolina; Asheville Communities Network catalogued 41 intentional communities in beginning stages in WNC alone as of 2025, indicating exceptional pipeline density.
properties.migration_notes: Regen-driven and climate-haven in-migration has driven land prices upward; the region has 30 years of institutional depth attracting practitioners.
properties.rural_density_notes: Western NC 18-county regional density ~47 persons/km2 per WNC Vitality Index 2022; Buncombe County (Asheville hub) ~120-158/km2; surrounding rural counties (Haywood, Madison, Yancey, Mitchell, Avery) 15-40/km2.
properties.source: accessibility.md, WNC Vitality Index and US Census 2020
properties.gaps: Dossier does not give explicit median age data for WNC.

properties.population_trend_notes: Dossier describes density and infrastructure but does not give an explicit recent population trend direction for the core Driftless counties.
properties.rural_density_notes: Driftless core counties per 2020 Census: Vernon ~15.0 persons/km2, Crawford ~10.9/km2, Richland ~11.3/km2; 5-county regional average ~18.9/km2.
properties.source: accessibility.md, US Census Bureau 2020 Decennial Census
properties.gaps: Dossier omits explicit recent population trend, median age, and migration figures for the Driftless core.

properties.population_trend_notes: Most Ozark counties have lost population in every census since 1980; Newton County had ~7,600 in 2020 down from peaks of ~13,000 in the early 20th century; Searcy County ~7,800 in 2020.
properties.median_age_notes: Older median age 45-50 in core counties per accessibility.md demographic profile.
properties.migration_notes: Outmigration of working-age adults dominant since 1980; Starlink-enabled first wave of knowledge-economy workers relocating to the region is a positive demographic signal but not enough to reverse decline.
properties.rural_density_notes: Newton County, AR: ~3.4 persons/km2; Searcy County: ~4.6/km2; Stone County: ~5.8/km2 per US Census 2020; all JRC GHSL fully rural.

properties.population_trend_notes: Dossier does not give explicit recent population trend direction for Taos County.
properties.migration_notes: Dossier notes earthship and arts/tourism economy attract practitioners and inflate land values via earthship B and B and short-term rental operators, but does not quantify recent net migration.
properties.rural_density_notes: Taos County population density 6.05 persons/km2 per US Census 2020 (population 34,489; land area 5,710 km2); 54.6% of residents live in rural areas.
properties.gaps: Dossier omits explicit recent population trend, median age, and net migration data for Taos County.

properties.population_trend_notes: Dossier provides density and economic context but does not give an explicit recent population trend direction for Nova Scotia or Cape Breton.
properties.migration_notes: Immigration-friendly context noted (international collaborators can join within 1-2 year pathway); NS PNP quota cut 50% in 2025 is referenced in MEMORY but not visible in these dossier files.
properties.rural_density_notes: Nova Scotia provincial density 18.4 persons/km2 per Statistics Canada 2021 Census; Cape Breton Island overall ~38.7/km2 but Inverness County only ~6.1/km2; rural Cape Breton Highlands 2-5/km2.
properties.gaps: Dossier omits explicit recent population trend, median age, and migration figures for Nova Scotia.

properties.population_trend_notes: Dossier covers density and intentional-community depth but does not give an explicit recent population trend direction for the Central Kootenay.
properties.rural_density_notes: Regional District of Central Kootenay density 2.8 persons/km2 per Statistics Canada 2021 Census (62,509 people over 22,265 km2); electoral sub-districts range from 0.3/km2 to 23.8/km2; Nelson city ~820/km2.
properties.gaps: Dossier omits explicit recent population trend, median age, and migration figures for the Kootenays.

properties.population_trend_notes: Dossier covers density and accessibility but does not give an explicit recent population trend direction for Estrie.
properties.migration_notes: Proximity to Montreal creates second-home demand pushing rural land prices upward through 2000s-2020s; no explicit migration data given.
properties.rural_density_notes: Estrie administrative region ~33 persons/km2 per Statistics Canada 2021 Census (337,701 residents over 10,212 km2); Sherbrooke core >1,000/km2 with agricultural rural areas 5-20/km2.
properties.gaps: Dossier omits explicit recent population trend, median age, and migration figures for Estrie.

properties.population_trend_notes: Dossier provides 2020 census totals and municipal fragmentation context but does not give an explicit recent population trend direction for the highlands.
properties.rural_density_notes: Oaxaca state overall ~44 hab/km2 per INEGI Census 2020 (4,132,148 people over 93,758 km2); Valles Centrales jurisdiction 80.8/km2; Sierra Norte jurisdiction 22.9/km2.
properties.source: accessibility.md, INEGI Censo de Poblacion y Vivienda 2020
properties.gaps: Dossier omits explicit recent population trend, median age, and net migration data for Oaxaca highlands.
